---
title: "Skills"
description: "How my research program is built—methods, computation, and rigor—beyond a generic résumé."
format:
  html:
    output-file: index.html
    css:
      - ../assets/css/research-skills.css
---

```{=html}
<div class="skills-scan-section">

  <div class="skills-group">
    <h4 class="skills-group-label">
      <i class="fas fa-flask"></i> Research Methods
    </h4>
    <div class="skills-group-badges">
      <span class="skill-badge">Psychophysics</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Pupillometry</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Eye Tracking</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">EEG</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">fMRI (Collaborations)</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Handgrip Dual-Task Paradigms</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Psychometric Function Modeling</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Within-Subject Design</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Preregistration</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">NASA-TLX</span>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="skills-group">
    <h4 class="skills-group-label">
      <i class="fas fa-chart-line"></i> Statistics & Modeling
    </h4>
    <div class="skills-group-badges">
      <span class="skill-badge">Hierarchical Bayesian DDM</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Mixed-Effects Models (LMM / GLMM)</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Signal Detection Theory</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Bayesian LBA (PyMC)</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">XGBoost / ML</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Platt Scaling / Calibration</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Sensitivity Analysis</span>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="skills-group">
    <h4 class="skills-group-label">
      <i class="fas fa-code"></i> Programming
    </h4>
    <div class="skills-group-badges">
      <span class="skill-badge">R (tidyverse, ggplot2, lme4, brms)</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Python (PyMC, pandas, scipy)</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">MATLAB / PsychToolbox</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Stan / CmdStan</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">React / TypeScript</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">R Shiny</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Git / GitHub</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Quarto / R Markdown</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">LaTeX</span>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="skills-group">
    <h4 class="skills-group-label">
      <i class="fas fa-vr-cardboard"></i> XR / HCI
    </h4>
    <div class="skills-group-badges">
      <span class="skill-badge">XR Interaction Design</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Gaze Interaction</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Hand Tracking</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Adaptive UI Policies</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Remote Study Design</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Fitts' Law / ISO 9241-9</span>
      <span class="skill-badge">Midas Touch Problem</span>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
<hr class="skills-divider">
<p class="skills-detail-label">
  <i class="fas fa-chevron-down"></i> 
  Detailed methodology notes for technical reviewers below
</p>
```

::: {.page-lead}
This page describes how the [Research](../research/research.qmd) program is implemented—methods, workflow, and computation—not a generic résumé. The dissertation toolkit fills the sections below; XR and interaction methods are grouped at the end as adjacent work.
:::

::: {.link-row}
[Publications](../publications/publications.qmd)
[CV](../cv.qmd)
:::


---

```{=html}
<div class="section-card">
```

## Core research methods

::: {.skills-block}
### Psychophysics

Design and analysis of auditory and visual same–different discrimination (and related tasks) under load; linking physical effort to sensitivity, bias, and dynamics.

### Pupillometry

Preprocessing and quality control (blinks, artifacts, valid samples); window-specific validity; gap-aware handling of missing samples.

### Interpretation

Pupil measures inform arousal and effort hypotheses; behavior and formal models remain primary for inference (aligned with [Research](../research/research.qmd)).
:::

```{=html}
</div>
```

---

```{=html}
<div class="section-card">
```

## Experimental design and data collection

::: {.skills-block}
### Handgrip dual-task paradigm

Concurrent physical effort (graded isometric grip) with cognitive and perceptual tasks, including same–different discrimination and memory components as required.

### Within-subject structure and preregistration

Factorial and repeated-measures layouts; counterbalancing of orders and conditions. Where studies are preregistered, analyses follow registered hypotheses, exclusions, and plans.

### Lab implementation

MATLAB and PsychToolbox for stimulus delivery and responses; calibration and exclusion rules documented in materials.
:::

```{=html}
</div>
```

---

```{=html}
<div class="section-card">
```

## Modeling and statistical inference

::: {.skills-block}
### Psychometric function modeling

Separate effects on perceptual evidence versus criterion under physical effort and load.

### Mixed-effects models

LMMs and GLMMs in R (lme4, emmeans) for repeated measures and individual differences, aligned with the [dual-task roadmap](../projects/dual-task-case-study.qmd).

### Hierarchical Bayesian Wiener DDM (dissertation)

Trial-level decomposition (drift rate, boundaries, non-decision time); implemented with brms and Stan (CmdStan-class) workflows. Scope and caveats—non-decision time constrained by design; pupil–parameter links exploratory—are stated on [Research](../research/research.qmd).

### Model scrutiny

Sensitivity analyses, alternative specifications, model comparison, and interval- or equivalence-style reasoning where relevant.
:::

::: {.xr-note}
The [XR case study](../projects/case_study_web.qmd) uses hierarchical Bayesian LBA in PyMC for verification-phase RTs after target entry. That framework is separate from the dissertation Wiener DDM and is not interchangeable with it.
:::

```{=html}
</div>
```

---

```{=html}
<div class="section-card">
```

## Programming and research computing

::: {.skills-block}
### R

tidyverse, ggplot2; mixed models; brms and Stan interfaces; Quarto for analyses and this site.

### MATLAB

PsychToolbox for experiment control.

### Version control

Git and GitHub for versioned scripts and reproducible layout.
:::

```{=html}
</div>
```

---

```{=html}
<div class="section-card">
```

## Scientific communication and reproducibility

::: {.skills-block}
### Reproducible reports

Quarto and R Markdown (legacy) so tables and figures rebuild from code.

### Writing and rigor

LaTeX for manuscripts; exploratory versus confirmatory reporting kept distinct where both apply.

### Traceability

README notes, pinned dependencies where used, and QC logs for pupillometry and behavioral pipelines.
:::

```{=html}
</div>
```

---

```{=html}
<div class="section-card">
```

## Additional and adjacent methods

::: {.panel-adjacent}
### Hand versus gaze interaction (portfolio / preprint)

[arXiv:2603.15991](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15991) · [case study](../projects/case_study_web.qmd). Adjacent to the dissertation; adds HCI- and XR-relevant breadth.

- Web task: React and TypeScript; remote sessions; display calibration and session checks across devices.
- Design: ISO 9241-9–style multidirectional tapping; Fitts difficulty and throughput; Williams block counterbalancing; NASA-TLX workload.
- Gaze proxy: physiologically informed gaze simulation (latency, jitter, saccadic suppression) for gaze versus hand comparison without lab eye-tracking.
- Policy-triggered adaptive UI: declutter (gaze) and width inflation (hand). In the analyzed data, only declutter executed and was evaluable (modest timeout reduction; slips still dominated gaze errors). Hand width inflation was not evaluable—targets did not scale in the UI (integration bug); that policy is not validated here.
- LBA fits: Python and PyMC for verification-phase RTs only—see the note under Modeling above.

Other secondary experience: [surgeon dashboard](../projects/surgeon-performance-predict2.qmd) (R Shiny, XGBoost); [EEG thesis](../projects/memory-and-meditation.qmd); imaging collaborations under [Publications](../publications/publications.qmd); SST role on [About](../about/about.qmd).
:::

```{=html}
</div>
```

---

::: {.section-card}

## Languages

::: {.lang-chips}
[Persian — Native]{.lang-chip}
[English — Professional]{.lang-chip}
[French — Basic]{.lang-chip}
:::

:::
